mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive corporate earnings expectations and higher equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening supply-side capacity, which supports corporate output and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns over 60 days in expansion regimes, with Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple expansion cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in housing starts signals immediate construction sector momentum and consumer confidence, generating short-term positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Housing starts momentum yields negative forward returns in >60% of observations, or 5-day return correlation falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a high federal funds rate creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payrolls growth exceeding 200,000 jobs per month demonstrates strong labor demand and economic resilience, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Payrolls growth above threshold fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with hit rate below 55% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration below 1.5% during a late-cycle phase signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity market repricing and negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration triggers positive forward returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in late-cycle regimes.
